lastReleaseMessage: docs: sync DEPLOY webhook path + PROJECT_SPEC migration/renderer refs

barRaise.topFinding: Full pass, not carry-forward: the skip-unchanged gate failed on tree state. HEAD is still 4dd1546 with zero commits since 2026-07-18, but this morning's audit found the icon field undocumented in PROJECT_SPEC.md and drift-auto-fix applied the doc edit, so the tree now carries two uncommitted files instead of one. That is the real shape of the problem: the audit chain finds drift and fixes it correctly, then nothing ships it (src/main.js stranded 10 days, PROJECT_SPEC.md joined today). Both hard rules pass and all three suites are green (9 integrity checks, 31/0 migration, 71/0 render). New this run: .project-context.json tags read browser-app, celtic, label-designer, none of which map to a domain-lens folder, so a by-the-book Wave 4 mapping skips all 18 domain lenses and reviews this project on 12 dimensions instead of 30; the status JSON carries the four lens tags the fan-out actually uses, so the two files disagree about the review surface. Health dips one point to 84 on maintainability and two orchestration handoff dimensions, offset by data-integrity and audit-loop gains. Three findings now chronic at runsOpen 7.

actions.title: .project-context.json tags (browser-app, celtic, label-designer) match no domain-lens folder, so a by-the-book Wave 4 mapping skips all 18 domain lenses; the status JSON carries the four lens tags the runs actually use. Add the lens tags to the context file, matching the BrackishRising pattern.

Stranded working tree grew from one file to two: src/main.js (v1.0.4 to v1.1.0 comment stamp, uncommitted 10 days) and PROJECT_SPEC.md (today's icon documentation). Both correct, neither shipped. One release.ps1 run clears both.

scripts/discord-notify.ps1:5-16 header still names the dead in-repo webhook path and two retired webhook names (Apothecary Push, YesAndApothecary Headline Bot), contradicting line 39 of the same file and the naming standard at CLAUDE.md.

tensionsOpen.title: The audit chain applies correct doc fixes but is forbidden from releasing them, so correct edits accumulate uncommitted. Neither auto-releasing nor stopping the fixes is right; the gap belongs to a release trigger.
